channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Deep hole almost middle of the road, 30th June
item.description:
This is a really dangerous hole. You can miss it when you turn out then your wheel goes down into it causing a distraction to you as a driver. I’ve reported this before yet it doesn’t appear as having been reported. Please fix it it’s a nasty hole.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southend Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 30th June
item.description:
Fly tipping in the usual spot by No 4 the sublet HMO.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Talbot Road, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On Provost Road on the same side of the road opposite no 9/10, 30th June
item.description:
The Street light is not working (same issue further up the road but with street light on the other side has been reported just now)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eton Villas, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near 14 Downside Crescent, 30th June
item.description:
Two extremely large potholes

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Downside Crescent, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car in the middle of the road causing danger, 30th June
item.description:
There has been a car in the middle of the road since 5pm and I am worried this is going to cause an accident. Cars cannot get around it without mounting the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Merewood Road, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light opposite side of the road from no 19 Provost Road, 30th June
item.description:
The street light is not working (the one opposite no 16 was fixed, this is further up the road but on the same side)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eton Villas, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 8-10 inches of grass on The Grove grass verges, 30th June
item.description:
Since Thames Water arrived end of May, the grass verges in The Grove, Sidcup have not been cut for 4 weeks. The maintenance of the water pipes are not affecting all of The Grove verges at one time so please can the areas outside Nos 35 to 102 please be cut asap.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 91, The Grove, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Commercial litter, 30th June
item.description:
Commercial littler from shop and overflowing bins contributing to litter gathering. Not picked up by nearby shop or litter pickers

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Monteagle Way, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Full bin, 30th June
item.description:
Overflowing bin. Bin men did not fully collect all bins last week and now overflowing. Other residents In houses using the communal flat bins contributing to it overflowing.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Monteagle Way, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Charging cable across pavement, 30th June
item.description:
I wish to report a recurring trip hazard on the public footway outside 59 Park End, Bromley. The occupier appears to charge an electric vehicle by routing a charging cable through a purpose-built opening in the front boundary wall. The cable is then laid across the public pavement within a protective cable cover before connecting to a vehicle parked on the highway. Whilst I appreciate that the cable is enclosed within a protective case, it nevertheless creates a raised obstruction across the footway. The cable typically only appears during the evening, when visibility is naturally reduced, making the obstruction more difficult for pedestrians to see. My child has now tripped over this cable on several occasions whilst walking along the pavement. Fortunately, no serious injury has occurred to date, but I am increasingly concerned that it presents an ongoing hazard to members of the public, particularly young children, elderly pedestrians and those with visual or mobility impairments. I would be grateful if the Council could investigate this arrangement to determine whether it complies with the London Borough of Bromley’s requirements for cross-pavement electric vehicle charging and whether any action is required to ensure the public footway remains safe and unobstructed. Thank you for your attention to this matter And I look forward to hearing from you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park End, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple Lengths of Unwanted Carpet Dumped on Pavement, 30th June
item.description:
Multiple lengths of unwanted carpet have been left dumped on the pavement outside number 37. Can the local team please remove it. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Waverley Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet
